summary: Refreshed webspa-admin's login page, authenticated shell, dashboard, tenant catalog, and cadence studio into a more cohesive operator UI while preserving existing data fields, actions, routes, dependencies, and backend behavior.

acceptance_results.criterion: Dashboard renders existing KPI data in a clearer operator command-center layout.
acceptance_results.reason: The dashboard still uses the existing KPI response fields and adds a bounded percent-width helper for funnel meters.

acceptance_results.criterion: Tenant catalog create/edit and member management preserve existing actions while improving hierarchy and scanability.
acceptance_results.reason: Existing form bindings, save/filter/member/deactivate handlers, table columns, and paginator bindings are preserved.

acceptance_results.criterion: Global shell/header/sidebar and login polish keep existing CoreUI and Angular Material dependencies.
acceptance_results.reason: No dependency manifests changed; the dev login route was screenshot-tested with Chromium after a clean dev-server restart.

acceptance_results.criterion: Cadence management is visually aligned with the refreshed authenticated admin shell.
acceptance_results.reason: The cadence route now uses the same hero, control card, rounded tabs, and operator hierarchy as the refreshed dashboard and tenant pages.

cleanup_notes: Hydrated frontend dependencies with npm ci --legacy-peer-deps in the isolated worktree; dependency manifests were unchanged.
Removed a stale empty .agent/tasks.json.lock generated during harness task creation.
A dev server is running at http://127.0.0.1:4210 for operator review.
completed_work: Created TMP-071 issue and work order with HVC allow classification.
Wrote a Mythos packet under .agent-work for the UI refresh.
Reworked the login page into a branded operator access screen.
Reworked the dashboard into a command-center layout using existing KPI fields and conversion rates.
Reworked the tenant catalog visual hierarchy while preserving current create/update/filter/member workflows.
Reworked cadence management into a cadence studio layout and fixed authenticated-shell visual collisions found during token-backed screenshots.
Polished global shell/header/sidebar tokens away from the generic default palette.
known_gaps: Authenticated routes were browser-screenshotted by seeding local Auth0 cache with the provided bearer token.

Local tenant and cadence API GETs are blocked in Chromium because localhost:8084 and localhost:8091 return 204 preflight responses without Access-Control-Allow-* headers; direct curl with Authorization plus tenant context succeeds for the tested endpoints.
Dashboard data remains unavailable locally because http://localhost:5001 was not listening during verification.
The Angular build still reports pre-existing campaign SCSS budget and selector warnings unrelated to TMP-071.
The local shell uses Node v24.15.0 while webspa-admin declares Node ^18.19.0 || ^20.9.0; npm reports EBADENGINE as a warning during hydration.
operator_message: TMP-071 is ready for review: webspa-admin now has a refreshed login, shell, dashboard, and tenant catalog UI with existing behavior preserved.
risk_notes: The UI changes are presentation-level and do not change API calls or tenant workspace behavior.
Dashboard funnel bars clamp display width to 0-100 while retaining the exact numeric percentage text.

validation.output_excerpt: ok true; existing schedulable/historical warnings only

validation.command: curl OPTIONS preflight for localhost:8084 and localhost:8091 from Origin http://localhost:4210
validation.output_excerpt: Both endpoints returned 204 with no Access-Control-Allow-* headers, matching Chromium net::ERR_FAILED.
